title:
Lesson 1:
I have, You have
description: 'Var' means 'there is/have'. 'Bende var' = I have, 'Sende var mı?' = Do you have? Turkish doesn't use 'to have' the same way as English.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Gerçekten mi? Benim bir kedim var.

title:
Lesson 2:
Colors
description: Basic colors: 'kırmızı' (red), 'mavi' (blue), 'yeşil' (green). Turkish adjectives don't change with gender.

title:
Lesson 3:
I like
description: 'Sevmek' = to like/love. 'Ben futbolu severim' = I like soccer. 'Hoşlanmak' is also used for 'to like'.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I like it a lot.

title:
Lesson 4:
Common Actions
description: Present tense verbs. 'Konuşmak' (to speak): 'konuşurum' = I speak, 'konuşursun' = you speak. 'Çalışmak' (to study/work).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Yes, I speak English and Turkish.

title:
Lesson 5:
Working
description: 'Çalışmak' (to work): 'çalışıyorum' = I work, 'çalışıyorsun' = you work. Used for both work and study.

title:
Lesson 6:
To read
description: 'Okumak' (to read): 'okuyorum' = I read, 'okuyorsun' = you read. Talk about reading books, magazines, etc.

title:
Lesson 7:
To write
description: 'Yazmak' (to write): 'yazıyorum' = I write, 'yazıyorsun' = you write.

title:
Lesson 8:
The weather
description: Weather expressions: 'Hava sıcak' = It's hot, 'Hava soğuk' = It's cold, 'Yağmur yağıyor' = It's raining, 'Kar yağıyor' = It's snowing.

title:
Lesson 9:
Clothing
description: Clothing vocabulary: gömlek (shirt), pantolon (pants), ayakkabı (shoes), elbise (dress). 'Giymek' = to wear.

title:
Lesson 10:
Body parts
description: Basic body parts: baş (head), gözler (eyes), eller (hands), ayaklar (feet). 'Ağrımak' = to hurt.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I'm not well. My head hurts.

title:
Lesson 11:
The house
description: House vocabulary: ev (house), oda (room), mutfak (kitchen), banyo (bathroom), oturma odası (living room).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: The bathroom is to the right.

title:
Lesson 12:
Animals
description: Common animals: köpek (dog), kedi (cat), kuş (bird), balık (fish), at (horse).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Yes, I like them a lot.

title:
Lesson 13:
At school
description: School vocabulary: okul (school), sınıf (class), kitap (book), kalem (pencil), öğretmen (teacher).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: My book is in the class.

title:
Lesson 14:
To need
description: 'İhtiyacı olmak' / 'Lazım olmak' (to need): 'İhtiyacım var' = I need, 'İhtiyacın var' = you need.

title:
Lesson 15:
To want
description: 'İstemek' (to want): 'istiyorum' = I want, 'istiyorsun' = you want. Very common!

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you want to eat?

title:
Lesson 16:
To go
description: 'Gitmek' (to go): 'gidiyorum' = I go/I'm going, 'gidiyorsun' = you go/you're going.

title:
Lesson 17:
To do/make
description: 'Yapmak' (to do/make): 'yapıyorum' = I do/make, 'yapıyorsun' = you do/make. Very common!

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: What do you do on weekends?

title:
Lesson 18:
To see/watch
description: 'Görmek' (to see), 'İzlemek' (to watch): 'görüyorum' = I see, 'izliyorum' = I watch. Used for watching TV, movies, etc.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I watch television every day.

title:
Lesson 19:
There is/are
description: 'Var' means both 'there is' and 'there are'. Very useful for describing what exists or is available.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, there is a bank on the street.

title:
Lesson 20:
Question words review
description: Key question words: Ne? (what), Kim? (who), Nerede? (where), Ne zaman? (when), Neden? (why), Nasıl? (how), Ne kadar? (how much).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: She's at home. When are you going?

title:
Lesson 21:
Negation
description: To make a sentence negative, add '-me/-ma' or '-mı/-mu' to the verb. 'Konuşmuyorum' = I don't speak.

title:
Lesson 22:
My, your, his, her
description: Possessive suffixes: -im/-ım (my), -in/-ın (your), -i/-ı (his/her/its). 'Kitabım' = my book, 'Kitabın' = your book.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: His/Her books are on the table.

title:
Lesson 23:
Being review
description: Turkish uses suffix '-dir/-dır/-dur/-dür' for permanent states and location words for temporary location.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Yes, and I am very happy here.

title:
Lesson 24:
To open/close
description: 'Açmak' (to open): 'açıyorum' = I open. 'Kapamak' (to close): 'kapatıyorum' = I close.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What time do they open the store?

title:
Lesson 25:
Hobbies
description: Hobbies vocabulary: yüzmek (swim), koşmak (run), dans etmek (dance), şarkı söylemek (sing), resim yapmak (paint), yemek yapmak (cook).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you like to do?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I like to swim. And you?

title:
Lesson 26:
To play (sports)
description: 'Oynamak' (to play): 'oynuyorum' = I play, 'oynuyorsun' = you play. Use for sports and games.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I play soccer on Saturdays.

title:
Lesson 27:
To listen
description: 'Dinlemek' (to listen): 'dinliyorum' = I listen, 'dinliyorsun' = you listen.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What music do you listen to?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you listen to music every day?

title:
Lesson 28:
To understand
description: 'Anlamak' (to understand): 'anlıyorum' = I understand, 'anlıyorsun' = you understand.

title:
Lesson 29:
To know (facts)
description: 'Bilmek' (to know facts/information): 'biliyorum' = I know, 'biliyorsun' = you know.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you know where the bank is?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you know how to speak English?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I know how to speak English.

title:
Lesson 30:
To know (people/places)
description: 'Tanımak' (to know people/be familiar with): 'tanıyorum' = I know, 'tanıyorsun' = you know. Different from 'bilmek'!

title:
Lesson 31:
To be able/can
description: 'Abilmek/ebilmek' (can/to be able): 'yapabilirim' = I can do. Suffix added to verb stems.

title:
Lesson 32:
Frequency adverbs
description: Frequency words: her zaman (always), asla (never), bazen (sometimes), genellikle (generally), her gün (every day).

title:
Lesson 33:
Intensifiers
description: Intensifiers: çok (very/a lot), az (little/few), oldukça (quite), fazla (too much).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Me too. I work a lot.

title:
Lesson 34:
Conjunctions
description: Common conjunctions: ve (and), ama (but), veya (or), çünkü (because), -ken (when/while).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I like coffee but not tea.

title:
Lesson 35:
Adjective agreement
description: Turkish adjectives don't change with gender or number. They stay the same: yeni araba, yeni ev, yeni arabalar.

title:
Lesson 36:
Comparisons
description: Comparisons: 'daha... (than)' (more... than), 'daha az... (than)' (less... than), 'kadar' (as... as).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: The train is faster than the bus.

title:
Lesson 37:
This, that, these, those
description: Demonstratives: bu (this), şu (that - medium distance), o (that - far), bunlar (these), onlar (those).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Evet, ama şu kitabı tercih ederim.
dialogue.english: Yes, but I prefer that book.

title:
Lesson 38:
Near future
description: '-ecek/-acak' suffix = going to/will. 'Yiyeceğim' = I'm going to eat. Easy way to talk about future!

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What are you going to do tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Are you going to eat with us?

title:
Lesson 39:
Making plans
description: Using future tense to make plans: '-ecek/-acak', invitations, accepting/declining.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I'm going to the movies. Do you want to come?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: We're going to go on Saturday.

title:
Lesson 40:
At the doctor
description: Medical vocabulary: hasta (sick), ağrı (pain), ilaç (medicine), doktor (doctor), hastane (hospital).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: You should go to the doctor.

title:
Lesson 41:
Feeling words
description: Emotions: yorgun (tired), mutlu (happy), üzgün (sad), kızgın (angry), sinirli (nervous).

title:
Lesson 42:
Asking permission
description: '...abilir miyim?/...ebilir miyim?' = Can I...? / May I...? Polite way to ask for permission.

title:
Lesson 43:
Telephone phrases
description: Phone vocabulary: aramak (to call), telefon (phone), mesaj (message), konuşmak (to speak).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: No, she's not here. Who's speaking?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you want to leave a message?

title:
Lesson 44:
Describing things
description: Adjectives for things: büyük (big), küçük (small), yeni (new), eski (old), güzel (pretty), çirkin (ugly).

title:
Lesson 45:
Seasonal expressions
description: Seasons: ilkbahar (spring), yaz (summer), sonbahar (fall), kış (winter). Weather in each season.

title:
Lesson 46:
Daily routine
description: Routine verbs: kalkmak (get up), duş almak (shower), kahvaltı etmek (have breakfast), yatmak (go to bed).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What time do you get up?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you shower in the morning?

title:
Lesson 47:
Expressing preferences
description: 'Tercih etmek' (to prefer): 'tercih ediyorum' = I prefer. Use with nouns or infinitives.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Kahve mi çay mı tercih edersin?
dialogue.english: Do you prefer coffee or tea?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Okumayı mı televizyon izlemeyi mi tercih edersin?
dialogue.english: Do you prefer to read or watch television?

title:
Lesson 48:
Asking for opinions
description: Opinion phrases: 'Ne düşünüyorsun?' (What do you think?), 'Fikrin ne?' (What's your opinion?), 'Bence...' (I think that...).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you think of this movie?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What's your opinion of the restaurant?

title:
Lesson 49:
Giving suggestions
description: '-meli/-malı' (should): 'gitmelisin' = you should go. Giving advice.

title:
Lesson 50:
Expressing agreement
description: Agreement: 'Katılıyorum' (I agree), 'Haklısın' (You're right), 'Kesinlikle' (Exactly), 'Tabii' (Of course).

title:
Lesson 51:
Expressing disagreement
description: Disagreement: 'Katılmıyorum' (I don't agree), 'Sanmıyorum' (I don't think so), 'Aksine' (On the contrary).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, on the contrary, it's very exciting.

title:
Lesson 52:
Talking about abilities
description: 'Bilmek' + verb = to know how to do something. Talking about skills and abilities.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you know how to cook?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I know how to cook well.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you know how to play an instrument?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: No, I don't know how to play any instrument.

title:
Lesson 53:
Talking about experience
description: Using 'zaten/artık' (already), 'henüz' (not yet), 'hiç' (never) to talk about experiences.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: No, I have never visited Spain.

title:
Lesson 54:
Introducing the past
description: Simple past (-di/-dı/-du/-dü): konuştum (I spoke), konuştun (you spoke). Past tense endings.

title:
Lesson 55:
Past tense verbs
description: Past tense: yedim (I ate), yedin (you ate), gitti (he/she went). Regular past endings.

title:
Lesson 56:
Yesterday activities
description: Using past tense with 'dün' (yesterday), 'dün gece' (last night), 'geçen hafta' (last week).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Last night I studied a lot.

title:
Lesson 57:
Past tense: to go
description: 'Gitmek' in past tense: gittim (I went), gittin (you went), gitti (he/she went).

title:
Lesson 58:
Past tense: to do
description: 'Yapmak' in past: yaptım (I did), yaptın (you did), yaptı (he/she did).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What did you do on the weekend?

title:
Lesson 59:
Past tense: to have
description: Past with 'var': 'vardı' (there was/had). 'İyi bir günüm vardı' = I had a good day.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you have a good day?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I had an excellent day.

title:
Lesson 60:
Past tense: location
description: '-deydi/-daydı' (was at): evdeydim (I was at home), işteydim (I was at work).

title:
Lesson 61:
Talking about the past
description: Combining different past tense verbs to describe what happened. Building narrative skills.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Yesterday I went to the movies.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Yes, I liked it a lot.

title:
Lesson 62:
Reflexive verbs intro
description: Reflexive actions with 'kendi': kendimi yıkıyorum (I wash myself). Action done to oneself.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What time do you wake up?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you wash your face in the morning?

title:
Lesson 63:
Direct object pronouns
description: Direct object: -i/-ı/-u/-ü suffixes. 'Onu görüyorum' = I see him/her/it. Replace nouns.

title:
Lesson 64:
Indirect object pronouns
description: Indirect object: -e/-a suffixes. 'Ona verdim' = I gave to him/her. Show who receives action.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you give the book to Mehmet?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I gave him the book.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Will you lend me your pencil?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I'll lend you my pencil.

title:
Lesson 65:
Weather past tense
description: Talking about weather in the past: 'Hava sıcaktı' (It was hot), 'Yağmur yağdı' (It rained), 'Kar yağdı' (It snowed).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What was the weather like yesterday?

title:
Lesson 66:
Making invitations
description: Inviting someone: 'İster misin...?' (Do you want to...?), 'Gelmek ister misin?' (Would you like to come?).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you want to go to the concert?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I would like to go.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: When do you want to go?

title:
Lesson 67:
Accepting invitations
description: Accepting: 'Tabii!' (Sure!), 'Elbette!' (Of course!), 'Çok isterim' (I would love to), 'Mükemmel!' (Perfect!).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you want to come to my house?

title:
Lesson 68:
Declining invitations
description: Declining politely: 'Üzgünüm' (I'm sorry), 'Gelemem' (I can't), 'Belki başka zaman' (Maybe another day).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Bu gece sinemaya gitmek ister misin?
dialogue.english: Do you want to go to the movies tonight?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I have to study. Maybe another day.

title:
Lesson 69:
Expressing necessity
description: '-meli/-malı' = have to/must. 'Gitmeliyim' = I have to go. Expressing obligations.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you have to do today?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I need to finish a project.

title:
Lesson 70:
Sequencing events
description: Sequence words: önce (first), sonra (then/after), daha sonra (later), sonunda (finally), en son (lastly).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: First I went to the gym.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Then I went to the supermarket.

title:
Lesson 71:
Talking about duration
description: Duration: 'beri' (since/for), '-dir/-dır' (for), 'sırasında' (during). How long something has been happening.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: How long have you been studying Turkish?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I've been studying Turkish for a year.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I've been studying for six months.

title:
Lesson 72:
Expressing doubt
description: Expressing uncertainty: 'Emin değilim' (I'm not sure), 'Belki' (Maybe), 'Muhtemelen' (Probably).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Is it going to rain tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Are you going to the party?

title:
Lesson 73:
Review and celebration
description: Congratulations on reaching lesson 73! Review key concepts: greetings, verbs, time, daily life, past tense. Keep practicing!

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: We did it! We finished seventy-three lessons.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Evet, artık daha fazla Türkçe konuşuyorum.
dialogue.english: Yes, now I speak more Turkish.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: It's true. I am very happy.
